refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall support teleoperation with end-to-end communication latency compatible with safe remote operation.
refined_fr.rationale: The candidate FR is already focused on a single functional requirement: ensuring teleoperation latency is compatible with safe remote operation. It does not combine multiple obligations or include implementation details, thus it is atomic and solution-neutral.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure the end-to-end communication latency during teleoperation under agreed network conditions and verify that the 95th percentile latency (p95) is less than or equal to 150 ms, ensuring compliance with safety requirements.

candidate_dps: Implement a communication protocol optimized for low-latency end-to-end data transfer
Design a dedicated teleoperation control loop with prioritized data paths to minimize latency
Use local buffering and predictive control algorithms to compensate for communication delays
Establish a modular communication interface that abstracts network conditions and adapts dynamically to maintain latency requirements
selected_dp.selected_dp: Design a dedicated teleoperation control loop with prioritized data paths to minimize latency

potential_sources: Tight integration between communication and control modules
coupling_check.notes: The selected DP focuses on isolating the teleoperation control loop with prioritized data paths, which supports independence from other system functions and helps maintain latency requirements.

assumptions_risks: Network conditions remain within agreed parameters to meet latency targets
Operational policies do not introduce additional delays or constraints
Predictive control may be needed if latency approaches upper limits
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider explicitly including adaptive latency management mechanisms in the DP to handle variable network conditions and ensure consistent teleoperation safety.

im_boundary.name: Teleoperation
im_boundary.scope: Module responsible for remote control and teleoperation functions, including control loops and data prioritization

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Bi-directional data stream for vehicle state and sensor data

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Commands and control inputs from remote operator

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Communication channel for teleoperation data and control signals

module_containment_scores.notes: Very broad module; contains DP but with high diffusion and low cohesion due to encompassing many unrelated functions.
Vehicle module includes physical systems but teleoperation control loop is logically separate; would cause diffusion of teleoperation logic outside its natural boundary.
Highly cohesive containment of teleoperation control loop and prioritized data paths; minimal diffusion outside this module.
Safety services are related but do not primarily contain teleoperation control logic; would cause diffusion and poor cohesion.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: The Teleoperation module is the most specific and cohesive boundary to contain the dedicated teleoperation control loop with prioritized data paths, minimizing latency by localizing control logic and data handling within this module.
containment_risks: Potential interface complexity with Vehicle module for real-time data exchange.
Dependency on Network Link interface reliability may affect latency guarantees.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The DP directly addresses the FR by specifying a dedicated teleoperation control loop with prioritized data paths to minimize latency, which aligns with the requirement for end-to-end communication latency compatible with safe remote operation.

diffusion_risks: Potential interface complexity with Vehicle module for real-time data exchange.
Dependency on Network Link interface reliability may affect latency guarantees.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: The Teleoperation module shows high containment score (9) and low diffusion score (2), indicating strong cohesion and minimal diffusion of the teleoperation control loop and prioritized data paths within this module. This suggests a well-defined IM boundary that effectively encapsulates the DP.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification of the FR through the IM is feasible by measuring the teleoperation latency (p95 ≤ 150 ms) under agreed network conditions using the Teleoperation module's interfaces (Telemetry, Tasking, Network Link).
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: The allocation of the DP to the Teleoperation IM is clear and specific, with well-defined interfaces supporting the required data flows and control inputs necessary to meet the FR.

recommended_revisions: Monitor and manage interface complexity between Teleoperation and Vehicle modules to reduce integration risks.
Implement robust error handling and fallback mechanisms for the Network Link interface to maintain latency guarantees under variable network conditions.

decision.justification: All gates (FR->DP, DP->IM, FR->IM) have passed with strong justification and verification feasibility. The DP directly addresses the FR with a dedicated teleoperation control loop minimizing latency, and the IM boundary is well-defined with high cohesion and low diffusion. Although there are some interface complexity and network reliability risks, these are manageable through monitoring and robust error handling as recommended. Given the high safety criticality, the current level of decomposition sufficiently addresses the requirement with clear allocation and verification paths, so further sub-FR decomposition is not necessary at this stage.

revise.why: To implement recommended revisions focusing on managing interface complexity and enhancing error handling for network link reliability to maintain latency guarantees.

assumptions: The Teleoperation module can encapsulate the DP effectively within a single IM boundary.
Network conditions are within agreed parameters for latency verification.
open_risks: Interface complexity with Vehicle module may cause integration challenges.
Network Link interface reliability could impact latency guarantees under variable conditions.
evidence_needed: Latency measurements (p95 ≤ 150 ms) under operational network conditions.
Interface performance and error handling effectiveness between Teleoperation and Vehicle modules.
